sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.33 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Suppose that a finite$p$-group$G$has an abelian subgroup$A$of order$p^n$. Does$G$contain an abelian subgroup$B$of order$p^n$that is normal in$B^G$[r]{a)} if$p=3$? [r]{b)} if$p=2$? [r]{c)} If$p=3$and$A$is elementary abelian, does$G$contain an elementary abelian subgroup$B$of order$3^n$that is normal in$B^G$? The corresponding results have been proved for greater primes$p$, based on extensions of Th...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
